Nothing fixes my issues with audio, specifically a lack of dialogue and ambient music. title: Fallout 4

notes:
Everything is visually as it should be. The performance is good (1080p ~60, all ultra, godrays OFF), my mods all work.
I initially had a problem with the sound cutting out during certain events or after loud noises as others have noted. The sound hasn't crashed after applying some fixes. 

This is what I did to fix the sound:
1. If you haven't already installed xact in the game's prefix, put in a terminal without brackets: [WINEARCH=win64 WINEPREFIX='/home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx' winetricks xact]

2. Download the "faudio-19.01.tar.xz" here https://github.com/Kron4ek/FAudio-Builds/releases/tag/19.01
Extract it somewhere. 

3. Copy the .dll's from the .../faudio-19.01/x64 folder and paste them in /home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Fallout 4/prefix/drive_c/windows/system32/  Replacing the existing .dll's with the ones from that x64 folder. Make sure FAudio.dll is present. 

4. Put in a terminal without brackets: [WINEARCH=win64 WINEPREFIX='/home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx' winecfg]
Wait for the window to open and go to the Libraries tab. Add an override for xaudio2_7 and set it to native. Just native. This forces it to use the .dll we dropped in the prefix earlier.
Click apply and OK. 
After doing that my sound didn't break anymore. A bit of a different method than building a libFAudio.so and placing it the proton/dist folder, when I tried that, I had no sound. 

I also use the bBackgroundMouse=1 under the [Controls] section of Fallout4.ini to resolve the issue with the mouse not letting you turn all the way around.

notes:

title: Fallout 4

notes:
As previous reviewers have pointed out, you will probably need to install faudio19.01 and run winetricks xact in the game's Proton prefix. I've also added bBackgroundMouse=1 to my Fallout4Custom.ini. For running steam on my dedicated GPU, I had to use the steam-native-runtime package of my distro to circumvent the 'glXChooseVisual failed' error.

It runs almost out-of-the-box with only the Intel HD, but by all means, don't expect any playable framerates. I'm just adding this for the sake of completeness.

If you force WineD3D, the game will run just fine via primusrun. Godrays do not crash for me, but my laptop can hardly handle them anyway. Nonetheless, the performance is really solid and can roughly compare to running it on my Windows installation.

As a side note, I have even better performance by installing nvidia-xrun and starting steam-native on that Xorg session. This way, you don't need to use the WineD3D hack because you basically run the whole session on the dedicated GPU and "stream" it more seamlessly to the iGPU.

If the game crashes or has a black window only, add LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib/nvidia/libGL.so to the launch options. This fixed it for me.
title: Fallout 4

notes: The game performs really well just so long as you can live without music and speech. Just like Skyrim SE, this is fixable via the Faudio install mentioned below. Once Valve integrate a more recent version of Faudio into Proton, we should be good to go out of the box.

title: Fallout 4

notes:
I read a lot of people doing a lot of different things to get this game to work. For me, I am using a PS4 controller so I did not need to do the bBackgroundMouse fix as it is unnecessary.

The game functions pretty well out-of-the-box except for two major issues.

1) God-rays tank my FPS hard. I found there to be a mod on NexusMods called 'Ultra Quality God Rays Performance Fix' that worked wonders for me. It is a simple script that runs at the beginning of the game to change some God Ray parameters to keep it from absolutely destroying FPS. Of course, it is a mod and therefor it stops achievements. So turning them completely off is the preferred way to do that.

2) In-game audio does not work as intended. Without any fixes whatsoever, I find that the intro video (pre-menu, I mean) has sound, and everything else is dead silent. I tried to use the winetricks --force xact method and the WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" startup option but it seemed to only slightly remedy the bigger issue. After a few minutes sounds that were already playing (i.e. the radio or any other noise) would continue to completion and all audio would cease after that point. For me, I found this frustrating. I did find a fix, though. Some other people have mentioned something similar and I found it to work completely for me.

Download Kron4ek/FAudio-Builds, make wine_setup_faudio.sh runnable via chmod -x or right clicking and changing permissions, and run  WINEPREFIX="$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx" ./.steam/steam/steamapps/common/FAudio-Builds-master/wine_setup_faudio.sh
After this I had no more audio issues AND the issue of Fallout 4 hanging after exiting to desktop also resolved itself.

Hopefully this helps someone else who may be in the same boat as I was. After these fixes I have had no issues.

title: Fallout 4

notes:
TLDR;
1. Paste WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% into Steam Launch Settings.
Steam Launch Settings are found by Right Clicking the game and selecting properties.
2. Find Fallout4.ini by following ~/.steam/debian-installation/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout4. If /.steam doesn't show up in the directory, hit Ctrl+H, the folder is hidden and this will unhide it.
3. Disable God Rays by adding sStartingConsoleCommand=gr off under the [General] section of Fallout4.ini
4. Add bBackgroundMouse=1 at the bottom of the [Control] section of Fallout4.ini, unless you're using a controller.
5. If you play the game lower than the resolution of your desktop, ie 1080p native but run game at 900p, the mouse will get stuck on a certain point while going right or down. Either change the resolution of your desktop before launching, or run at native resolution. 
6. Exit via Main Menu or the game will have a high likelihood of crashing

notes:
So I originally posted a review of this with a gold rating on Ubuntu 18.04. On gentoo, the game had a black screen until esync was disabled. However the framerate was unstable and I had some screen tearing. Not sure if noesync and the tearing are related or not because I can't run it without the noesync option. notes: Put "WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%" in launch options. if you have a multi-monitor setup you have to unplug the monitor(s) that the game is not running on or your mouse will get stuck after turning
title: Fallout 4

notes: Use launch options "WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%" to fix audio, navigate to "/steam/folder/path/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout4/Fallout4.ini" and just under the "[Controls]" section (for me it was at the bottom of the file) set "bBackgroundMouse=1" to fix mouse speed issues. Finally, open the winecfg for the Proton prefix (I recommend using Protontricks, though you can export the WINEPREFIX in a Terminal session and do that too: https://github.com/Sirmentio/protontricks), go to the "Graphics" tab, make sure "Automatically capture the mouse in full-screen windows" is checked, and check "Emulate a virtual desktop." Set it to the resolution of the monitor you intend to play Fallout 4 on - For me, 1920x1080. Click apply, exit winecfg and winetricks, and the game will launch! I get a pretty consistent 60fps with max settings (and no performance boosting mods) after the game finishes loading. Tested walking around a little bit and some brief combat. Game crashes more often than not if you try to exit to desktop from the pause menu - Instead, exit to the main menu and then exit the game from there. I have not tried any mods but intend to in the future. Someone else made a report talking about using the Vortex mod manager on Linux and said they had success with mods, so it seems to be possible. Overall, I'm very impressed with how the game runs after a couple of minor tweaks!

Two problems initially: 1) Audio doesn't work . After setting launch option to "WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 STEAM_RUNTIME_HEAVY=1 %command%" the problem is resolved, but with a minor audio distortion sometimes, but not so bad that it ruins the experience.
2) Mouse is hyper-sensitive. This I couldn't fix, so keyboard and mouse is out of the question, but connecting and playing with my old Logitech Dual Action gamepad, the controls work flawlessly.
In all, the game is perfectly playable with good performance.
title: Fallout 4

notes:
First of all use this launch option: "WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%". Without the quotes of course. Also you should modify your Fallout.ini file to get rid of mouse issues. Add the following to the end of [Controls] in your ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My\ Documents/My\ Games/Fallout4/Fallout4.ini, "bBackgroundMouse=1". This should fix the worst. Unluckily the sound will eventually crash ingame and if you don't got a weapon in your Hands you may not look around correctly, your mouse will get stuck. I recommend on singe Monitor setups to not use the virtual desktop fix and use a native resolution of 1080p. Otherwise you will get a lot of fun trying to get the mouse to work. On multi Monitor setups you will need the protontricks virtual desktop fix. Protontricks is a really neat tool to help you fix minor issues with gaming on GNU/Linux. Read more about protontricks: https://github.com/Matoking/protontricks .  

My conclusion:
It it possible to play it on Linux but it dosen't feel amazing.
title: Fallout 4

notes:
I've set startup to `WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%` to fix the sound issues, and added "bBackgroundMouse=1" to Fallout4.ini to fix the mouse.

With those 2 tweaks, the game is fully playable. Graphics is smooth and task-switching works perfectly.

There are only two minor issues.

First, audio occasionally has very slight popping.

Second, mouse-look occasionally gets stuck inside a rectangle, preventing me from turning beyond about 90 degrees range. I can get it "unstuck" by switching between cursor and freelook mode (eg. open and close Pip Boy) several times while moving the mouse around.

Almost Gold rating.
title: Fallout 4

notes:
Runs perfectly smooth after setting the launch options to 'WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" %command%'.
This fixes any possible sound issues.
Since I'm using the fsync testing kernel, I don't have to disable esync.
title: Fallout 4

notes:
60FPS mostly on Medium settings. Drops to 45-55 while building settlements. Disabled God Rays, Motion Blur and Ambient Occlusion in the game launcher's advanced settings. High settings work in the mid 40's.
Ran: protontricks 377160 --gui - Installed FAudio to fix missing audio and changed sound to ALSA to fix the crackling audio. - Ran winecfg to enable a virtual desktop with my primary monitor's native resolution. - INI-Tweak: Track down your Fallout.ini in "$STEAMInstall/compatdata/377160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My\ Documents/My\ Games/Fallout4/Fallout4.ini", find the [Controls] section and add the line 'bBackgroundMouse=1' to fix the mouse issues. The mouse still needs to be clicked once in the game for it to be grabbed. - Steam launch options used: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% - Game crashes on occasion. If Alt+F4 doesn't bring up Proton's 'Terminate Process'-Dialogue and you kill it through your task manager, the VRAM will be left populated by the game data and you need to restart your system.
title: Fallout 4

notes: Although proton 4.11 has FAudio 19.07, I couldn't get audio to work with it, so I compiled FAudio with the flags -DXNASONG=OFF -DFFMPEG=ON, with the command "git clone git://github.com/FNA-XNA/FAudio.git && cd FAudio && mkdir build && cd build && cmake .. -DXNASONG=OFF -DFFMPEG=ON && make" then overwrited libFAudio.so.0 in the Proton 4.11 folder (dist/lib64). Also applied bBackgroundMouse=1 to the Fallout4.ini as everyone said. Careful with the xaudio option, it works but it's not definitive or perfect, the sound was still crackling a bit for me (clear crystal with FAudio), and if you try that, you need to delete the 377160 folder from the compatdata to try the FAudio.
title: Fallout 4

notes: The launcher comes up, but esync and other tweaks wouldn't stop the issue where it would crash when I hit play
title: Fallout 4

gpu: X.Org Gallium 0.4 on AMD HAWAII (DRM 2.50.0 / 4.19.0-0.bpo.5-amd64, LLVM 3.9.1)
notes: I'm using a nVidia GPU and a AMD one. On my AMD machine it was a bit more difficult to run Fallout4. I had to add another proton attribute but it is possible in only 2 Steps! STEP 1 -> set the following launch options "WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 STEAM_RUNTIME_HEAVY=1 PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%". Without the quotes of course. STEP 2 -> Modify your "/home/USERNAME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout4/Fallout.ini". Now we add just 2 little lines. The first line "sStartingConsoleCommand=gr off" goes right under [General]. The second line "bBackgroundMouse=1" goes to the end of [Control]. That should do the trick, have fun ;D
title: Fallout 4

notes: Using all the tweaks listed above and below makes the game run well enough. Sometimes I have audio pops and clicks when music is played or characters speak which is something I haven't been able to fix, however it only affects my client every once in a while so it is more manageable than I first thought. F4SE hasn't worked for me, and ENBs have been inconsistent as to whether they crash my game, but other than this, mods work well with the game, the performance is admirable.
title: Fallout 4

notes:
[MULTI-MONITOR FIXES] --> Oh boy, this was a hard one to fix... I have the following multi-monitor setup: 1380x720 monitor (Intel HDA) | 1080p (RX580) |  1080p (RX580)

I Added the usual fixes that you'll find in this page (Esync, bBackgroundMouse=1), and I edited winecfg to use a 1080p virtual desktop, but I couldn't move the mouse past 1/3 of the width of the screen. Aside from that, the game was unplayable. Yes, you could turn around, but once every second the mouse would snap to random edges of the virtual desktop.

[SOLUTION]: for some reason, wine stretched from the middle one to the left one, glitching like crazy. I killed the process and I saw for a glimpse the blue virtual desktop on like 10 pixels on the left monitor. After I disabled that monitor, everything works perfectly.
title: Fallout 4

notes: The mouse does not rotate 360 ​​degrees to the right after adding "Background Mouse = 1" in the Fallout4.ini file. Without adding "Background Mouse = 1" to the Fallout4.ini file, the mouse goes crazy. Using "WINEDLLOVERRIDES =" xaudio2_7 = n, b "PROTON_NO_ESYNC = 1% command%" the sound really works.
title: Fallout 4

notes: The game doesn't have sound. This can be fixed with: WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=90 %command% First one gives the sound and the later one is a general fix for Pulse's sound issues with Wine (crackling and stuff). If 90 msec latency doesn't work, try something else, like 60. Disabling esync isn't needed and it causes issues! The game might have some slight GPU-related performance issues, that are frequent with lower end GPU. I have quite old GPU (but above the minimum requirements), but I'd still suspect that sudden frame drops that sometimes happen for example when turning wouldn't happen on Windows on the lowest settings (I can't really check right now). Also occasional crashes.
title: Fallout 4

notes: Managed to test the game on Windows. It seems that the issue isn't framerate. On Proton with medium details I get 45-60 fps and no major drops. So instead of framerate drops the issue seems to be some stuttering. Without the stuttering the gameplay feels to be quite close to the Windows one, but now the performance is much worse. Also it is those stutters that cause crashes.

For me it runs better then windows now.

notes: GE version of Proton has newer FAudio and additional launch options aren't needed. GE-Proton can be downloaded from https://github.com/GloriousEggroll/proton-ge-custom/releases, then just untar the files to ~/.steam/steam/compatibilitytools.d/ (or according to wherever your steamfolder is) and force Steam or Fallout 4 use this version of Proton. Compared to 4.11 the performance seems to be a bit better, although on lower-end machine the game still suffers from framedrops (on Proton 4.10 the framecounter didn't give proper results which resulted misconception in my earlier reports). The framedrops are especially noticeable when using a weapon with recon-scope (that has some kind of special effect that turns the view grayshade), which drops the frames by 30%. The game is capped to 60 fps (but can be uncapped editing .ini potentially messing physics engine), so I'd guess that newer machines, which could potentially run the game with 100 fps or more, will experience quite little problems. On lower-end machine however, the game excperience is quite problematic.

title: Fallout 4

notes:
A easy to follow guide on how to get Fallout 4 to work on Linux using Steam Proton. 

Step 1 – Install Fallout 4 in Steam using Proton Version 4.11-3

Step 2 – Run Fallout 4 once and then exit to generate the ini files required for the below steps.

Step 3 – Download faudio from the below site (download latest version).

https://github.com/Kron4ek/FAudio-Builds/releases

Step 4 – Open up a terminal in the folder you extracted faudio to (the one with the x64 folder) and type in the below command.

WINEPREFIX="/home/ryan/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx" ./wine_setup_faudio.sh

Step 5 – Fix multi-monitor mouse turning issue.

Open up a terminal and type the below:

WINEARCH=win64 WINEPREFIX='/home/ryan/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx' winecfg

Under the Graphics tab, tick ‘automatically capture the mouse in full-screen window’ and ‘emulate a virtual desktop’

Set the desktop size as 1920 x 1080 (or your resolution).

Step 6 - Fix mouse and lighting issue

Navigate to Home / .steam / steam / steamapps / compatdata / 377160 / pfx / drive_c / users / steamuser / My Documents / My Games / Fallout4

Open up Fallout4Pref.ini and edit the below to fix the lighting problem. 

[Display]
bComputeShaderDeferredTiledLighting=0

Create a new Fallout4Custom.ini with the below to fix the mouse issue. 

[Controls]
bBackgroundMouse=1

REMAINING BUGS 

Exiting direct to desktop will crash the game, so exit to main menu and then exit to desktop.

Pressing TAB does not open up the Pip-Boy, so reassign this to another key instead. 

See https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pWJPfGGy9KY&t=2s for a visual tutorial.
title: Fallout 4

notes: The light glitch has been fixed, Fixed the mouse by adding 'bBackgroundMouse=1' to Fallout4.ini and run well with this line added to the Launch Options in Steam: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=90 PROTON_LOG=1 %command%
title: Fallout 4

notes: Added bComputeShaderDeferredTiledLighting=0 to the Fallout4Prefs.ini and added Fallout4Custom.ini with [Controls] bBackgroundMouse=1. Installed FAudio. Games works fine for a few minutes and then freezes.

The game tries to run for about 10 seconds but never, (I mean NEVER), makes it to the launcher. I even tried using proton 4.2.9, however I am completely lost in trying to get this game to run as it seems no one else has even the most remotely similar problem as me. Not worth the time at the moment.

WINEPREFIX="/home/ryan/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx" ./wine_setup_faudio.sh Step 5 – Fix multi-monitor mouse turning issue. notes: Fallout 4 DOES WORK with F4SE! To do this, download F4SE and install it like usual on Windows (f4se.silverlock.org). In the Fallout 4 game directory, move Fallout4Launcher.exe to another file and run "ln -s f4se_loader.exe Fallout4Launcher.exe" to link the F4SE launcher instead of the normal game launcher. After that, set your launch parameters (e.g. I use PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=90 PROTON_LOG=1 %command%) and play (with f4se mods of course). Unsure about ENB or ReShade though.

title: Fallout 4

notes:
Fallout 4 remains a work in progress with several issues outstanding. Using the PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=90 tweak now causes the game to crash whenever you attempt to load a save, so you're stuck with popping and crackling audio. If I try to run the game in anything less than my native resolution (1920x1080), the mouse is boxed in and I'm unable to turn a full 360 degrees without hitting an invisible wall. Running the game at native gives me wildly erratic frame rates (running happily at 60FPS only to suddenly plunge to 21FPS for no apparent reason, and lowering the graphics settings doesn't help), so it's not really an option. The game screams long without issue at 1280x720, but as mentioned above, the mouse is boxed in. The game is also plagued by sound lags, odd pauses and the occasional complete lock-up forcing a process kill from the terminal. Skyrim SE also featured some of the same sound problems (though PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=90 still works fine), but the game will run flawlessly in 1280x720 without Fallout 4's mouse issues, so I'm wondering what's going on there.

As it stands right now, I can't play Fallout4 without experiencing audio glitches or horrendous frame drops at native resolution.
title: Fallout 4

notes: Keyboard/Mouse user here, I had to make the modification of 'bBackgroundMouse=1' under  '[Controls]' in the 'Fallout4.ini' file that everyone else mentions.  Everything has been usable after that but I do feel that performance is slower than in Windows.  For me, it's playable at 1080p with high settings though.  The only persistent issue is I do occasionally have to 'Win-Tab' or 'Alt-Tab' back to the window to get the mouse to rotate fully around again, the 'invisible wall' effect seems to happen on initial startup, and also sometimes when interacting with a dialogue or menu with the mouse.  But it's very infrequent (other than at the start).  Becuase of that issue and the noticeably slower performance, I'd rate this as Silver rather than Gold.  Still very playable though.
title: Fallout 4

Fix mouse issue by adding bBackgroundMouse=1 under section [Control] in Fallout.ini,  which can be found in [steam install directory]/steam/compatdata/377160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My\ Documents/My\ Games/Fallout4
title: Fallout 4

notes:
A word on the audio issues. Stable fix here. First you're gonna compile SDL2. See: https://www.libsdl.org/
You also need CMake for dependency later. 
Go here and build yourself some DLLs. Change the scripts to your needs, probably you only need to identify the correct proton version. https://github.com/Kron4ek/FAudio-Builds
Then you build FAudio, see: https://github.com/FNA-XNA/FAudio/wiki/FAudio-for-Proton
Ensure that the game's audio is being streamed by the SDL output. 
Viola. Enjoy. Bone atrophy. 
Now, I use the launch option string "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=100 %command%" only the latter portion is related to sound, I have no idea if this is necessary or does anything with the aforementioned changes, but since it's working I keep it there.
title: Fallout 4
